mission.title: node-exporter: Support new pressure interface
mission.description: Support new pressure interface. Requested by 10+ users.

steps.title: Check current node-exporter deployment
steps.description:
Verify your node-exporter version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n node-exporter -l app.kubernetes.io/name=node-exporter
helm list -n node-exporter 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working node-exporter installation.

steps.title: Review node-exporter configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant node-exporter configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n node-exporter -l app.kubernetes.io/name=node-exporter
kubectl get configmap -n node-exporter -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=node-exporter
```
Kernel 4.20 will include the [facebook psi pressure metrics](https://facebookmicrosites.github.io/psi/docs/overview) ([commit](https://git.kernel.org/pub/scm/linux/kernel/git/torvalds/linux.git/commit/?id=eb414681d5a07d28d2ff90dc05f69ec6b232ebd2)).

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support new pressure interface
steps.description:
This enables the collection of pressure stall information as exposed by the `/proc/pressure` interface added in the 4.20 release of the Linux kernel.
```yaml
/proc/pressure/cpu:1:some avg10=0.00 avg60=0.00 avg300=0.00 total=1167162866
/proc/pressure/io:1:some avg10=0.00 avg60=0.00 avg300=0.00 total=61955081
/proc/pressure/io:2:full avg10=0.00 avg60=0.00 avg300=0.00 total=58578187
/proc/pressure/memory:1:some avg10=0.00 avg60=0.00 avg300=0.00 total=0
/proc/pressure/memory:2:full avg10=0.00 avg60=0.00 avg300=0.00 total=0
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n node-exporter -l app.kubernetes.io/name=node-exporter
kubectl get events -n node-exporter --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support new pressure interface" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: This enables the collection of pressure stall information as exposed by the `/proc/pressure` interface added in the 4.20 release of the Linux kernel.
codeSnippets:
/proc/pressure/cpu:1:some avg10=0.00 avg60=0.00 avg300=0.00 total=1167162866
/proc/pressure/io:1:some avg10=0.00 avg60=0.00 avg300=0.00 total=61955081
/proc/pressure/io:2:full avg10=0.00 avg60=0.00 avg300=0.00 total=58578187
/proc/pressure/memory:1:some avg10=0.00 avg60=0.00 avg300=0.00 total=0
/proc/pressure/memory:2:full avg10=0.00 avg60=0.00 avg300=0.00 total=0

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with node-exporter installed or the issue environment reproducible.
